title: Slow Page Identification and Render Performance by Geography
dataSources: **Index:** `web` (dedicated retail/web-analytics index; partition by `host` or `site` if multi-brand). **Sourcetypes:** `access_combined` (Splunk TA–normalized Apache/Nginx combined format) or `nginx:access` / `apache:access` if you keep vendor-native extractions; optional `splunk_rum:web_vital` when ingesting Real User Monitoring (RUM) via HTTP Event Collector (HEC). **Input mechanisms:** (1) Universal Forwarder `inputs.conf` with `[monitor:///var/log/apache2/access.log]` or `[monitor:///var/log/nginx/access.log]` plus `host`, `source`, and `sourcetype` assignment in `props.conf` on the indexer/heavy forwarder tier; (2) HEC token on a heavy forwarder or Cloud HTTP endpoint for RUM JSON (`curl`/SDK posts with `Authorization: Splunk <token>`). **Key fields:** `uri_path` (requested path without query string; strip PII from query at transform layer); `response_time` — Apache `LogFormat` `%D` is **microseconds** (divide by 1000 for ms or normalize in `props.conf`); Nginx `$request_time` is **seconds** (multiply by 1000 for ms to align with this UC’s SPL, which treats stored values as seconds); `status` (HTTP status); `clientip` (originating client after `X-Forwarded-For` processing on the load balancer); `clientip_country` or `geoip_country` from **GeoIP** (`lookup geoip clientip OUTPUT clientip_country` at search time, or Splunk-approved index-time enrichment). **`http_user_agent`** for bot pruning. **Volume:** combined access logs typically **0.5–5 KB/event**; high-traffic retail fronts often land **50k–800k events/hour/site** depending on CDN offload (only origin hits ingest). Sample at the edge only if legally approved. **CIM:** Maps to Splunk **CIM Web** dataset when aliases tag `uri`/`uri_path`, `status`, client IP fields, and HTTP method (`method`/`http_method`) — accelerate only after stable field mapping; retail teams often query raw access before full CIM normalisation.
app: **Web logs:** Deploy Universal Forwarders on origin web nodes (or consolidate on a syslog/HEC aggregator tier). Parsing is typically handled by `Splunk_TA_nix` + `props.conf` for Apache/Nginx, or by `Splunk Add-on for Apache Web Server` (Splunkbase) / equivalent Nginx TA where your standard uses explicit extractions (`$deploymentserver`, `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/<ta>/`). **Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud 9.x+.** Install matching TA on indexers/heavy forwarders for parsing and on search heads only if tagging/CIM lookups are defined there. **HEC RUM:** Create an HEC token (Settings → Data inputs → HTTP Event Collector) with `index=web`, `sourcetype=splunk_rum:web_vital` (or your internal sourcetype), TLS enabled. Embed the JavaScript snippet from Splunk Observability / RUM documentation on storefront pages; events POST to `https://<collector>:8088/services/collector/event`. **Locations:** UF `etc/apps/<serverclass>/local/inputs.conf`; indexer `props.conf`/`transforms.conf` for field normalisation; SH `macros.conf` for environment-specific index names.
spl:
index=web sourcetype=access_combined
| eval response_ms=tonumber(response_time)*1000
| eval country=coalesce(clientip_country, geoip_country, "unknown")
| stats avg(response_ms) as avg_ms perc95(response_ms) as p95_ms count as hits by uri_path country
| where p95_ms > 3000 AND hits > 100
| sort - p95_ms
description: Ranks site paths and countries where server-reported response times show high averages and especially bad ninety-fifth percentile latency, after filtering for paths with enough traffic volume to be statistically meaningful.
value: Merchandising and digital teams can prioritize fixes where slow pages actually hurt the most shoppers—before cart abandonment shows up in sales reports—by steering engineering hours toward specific regions, content types, or edge-cache misconfigurations.
implementation: Ingest combined access logs with numeric `response_time` in seconds (convert to milliseconds in the search). Enrich `clientip` with a published GeoIP lookup at search time or index time. Exclude bots using `http_user_agent` filters. Pair this panel with separate real user monitoring feeds when you need paint and layout timing beyond server headers.
visualization: **Layout (Splunk Dashboard Studio or Classic XML, single row):** (1) **Panel A — “P95 latency heat”** — Horizontal bar chart: `country` on Y-axis, `p95_ms` on X; top 15 countries by p95; subtitle shows time range. (2) **Panel B — “Worst paths detail”** — Table: `uri_path`, `country`, `hits`, `avg_ms`, `p95_ms`; drilldown sets tokens `path_tok`/`country_tok`. (3) **Panel C — “Geographic tail”** — Choropleth / filled map on `country` with `p95_ms` (median or p95 rollup). (4) **Panel D — “Volume vs tail”** — Scatter: `hits` vs `p95_ms` by `uri_path` with point size = `hits`; separates low-traffic spikes from broad issues.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable unless you map web access into a web data model; most retail teams run this search directly on access logs.

references.title: Splunk Lantern — Use Case Explorer: Retail
Apache HTTP Server — Log files and mod_log_config (custom log formats)
Splunk Docs — HTTP Event Collector (HEC) setup

knownFalsePositives: (1) **Mobile-only regions with last-mile latency** — High p95 from access logs reflects network RTT and TLS handshakes, not server render time. *Mitigation:* Segment by `http_user_agent` device class, join RUM LCP/FCP where available, and compare p95 only for same ASN or Wi-Fi–heavy cohorts; raise thresholds for mobile-heavy countries. (2) **Single hero asset A/B tests** — One country bucket sees a full-screen video or uncompressed image, inflating `response_time` for `uri_path` matching only that experiment. *Mitigation:* Maintain an experiment lookup keyed on `uri_path` + `query` token; suppress or route alerts to growth team during active tests. (3) **Synthetic and SEO bot storms** — Crawlers and synthetic probes hit deep links with cold cache, skewing tail latency. *Mitigation:* Apply `lookup bots http_user_agent OUTPUT is_bot | where is_bot!=1` or use a maintained `ua_parser` macro; exclude known monitoring subnets via `cidrmatch`. (4) **CDN mis-attribution** — If logs are from origin only, a country field derived from edge IP differs from shopper geography behind the CDN. *Mitigation:* Prefer `True-Client-IP`/`CF-Connecting-IP` parsing into `clientip` before GeoIP; document whether country means origin-request geography vs shopper geography. (5) **Mixed time units on `response_time`** — Apache `%D` microseconds vs Nginx seconds mis-ingested without normalisation duplicates this UC’s false conclusions. *Mitigation:* enforce `EVAL-*` or `sed` in `props.conf` per sourcetype, add data-quality alert on `response_time > 120` seconds (likely microseconds). (6) **Burst traffic below `hits > 100` filter** — Legitimate incidents on low-traffic paths never surface; conversely `/health` spikes from load tests trip alerts. *Mitigation:* tune `hits` threshold per path tier via CSV lookup (`uri_tier=min_hits`).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud **9.x+**; Universal Forwarder on each origin web server (or a governed log shipper) with network path to indexers on **9997/tcp** (or cloud ingest URI).
• **Indexes:** `web` (or your standardised retail index); **roles** grant `srchIndexesAllowed` including that index for merchandising and SRE roles—avoid over-broad `admin` grants on PII-bearing access logs.
• **Time sync:** NTP/chrony on all web nodes; skew >30s breaks correlation with deploy markers and CDN purge events.
• **Field contract:** `uri_path`, `response_time`, `status`, `clientip` (or `remote_ip` aliased), and **GeoIP** enrichment path documented (search-time `lookup` vs index-time pipeline).
• **Unit discipline:** Confirm whether `response_time` is stored in **seconds** (Nginx `$request_time`), **milliseconds**, or **microseconds** (Apache `%D`). The bundled SPL multiplies by 1000 assuming **seconds** → **milliseconds**; misconfiguration here is the #1 false positive driver—see Step 5.
• **Bots / synthetics:** Maintain `bots_subnets.csv` or Splunk **URL Toolbox** / UA parsing macro to strip non-customer traffic.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
On the **Universal Forwarder**, create `etc/apps/retail_web_uf/local/inputs.conf`:

```ini
[monitor:///var/log/nginx/access.log]
disabled = 0
host = $HOSTNAME
index = web
sourcetype = access_combined

[monitor:///var/log/apache2/access.log]
disabled = 0
host = $HOSTNAME
index = web
sourcetype = access_combined
```

On the **indexer or heavy forwarder** parsing tier, normalise extractions in `props.conf` (example patterns—adjust to your `log_format`):

```ini
[access_combined]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TIME_FORMAT = %d/%b/%Y:%H:%M:%S %z
TRANSFORMS-dns = force_path_for_uri
```

Use `transforms.conf` to copy WAF-trusted client IP into `clientip` **before** GeoIP. **HEC RUM path:** create an HEC global or per-token stanza with `indexes=web`, `disabled=0`, TLS enabled; POST JSON events that include paint/timing fields for future joins—keep separate sourcetype for clarity (`splunk_rum:web_vital`).

**Verification SPL:** `index=web earliest=-15m sourcetype=access_combined | stats count avg(len(_raw)) as avgbytes by host` — nonzero counts across all storefront hosts; investigate any host lagging more than five minutes (`_time` vs `now()`) using `_indextime` lag stats.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
```spl
index=web sourcetype=access_combined
| eval response_ms=tonumber(response_time)*1000
| eval country=coalesce(clientip_country, geoip_country, "unknown")
| stats avg(response_ms) as avg_ms perc95(response_ms) as p95_ms count as hits by uri_path country
| where p95_ms > 3000 AND hits > 100
| sort - p95_ms
```

Understanding this SPL
• **Base filter** restricts to storefront access lines in `web` + `access_combined`.
• **`tonumber(response_time)*1000`** converts vendor **seconds** (typical combined log) into **milliseconds** for human thresholds comparable to Core Web Vitals discussions. If your pipeline stores **microseconds**, remove the `*1000` or divide accordingly—verify with `| stats min(response_time) max(response_time)` on a known path.
• **`coalesce(clientip_country, geoip_country, "unknown")`** picks the first non-null regional dimension; if both null, search-time `| lookup geoip clientip as clientip OUTPUT country as clientip_country` can be prepended once `clientip` is trustworthy.
• **`stats ... by uri_path country`** aggregates all requests in the window: **avg** shows sustained pain, **perc95** captures tail shopper experience, **count** is sample depth.
• **`where p95_ms > 3000 AND hits > 100`** enforces both **severity** (3s p95 is poor for HTML document requests) and **statistical confidence** (noise paths with two visits drop out).
• **`sort - p95_ms`** surfaces the worst geo/path combinations for triage order.

Schedule as Alert: cron **`0 * * * *`** (hourly) or **`*/15 * * * *`** for peak season; time range **`-65m@m` to `-5m@m`** to avoid partial minutes; trigger **number of results > 0**; throttle **4h** on composite key `uri_path+country`; severity **medium** unless tied to checkout prefix `/checkout` macro (then **high**).

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Pick one result row; run `index=web uri_path="<path>" country="<c>" earliest=-24h | timechart span=1h perc95(response_ms)` — spike should align with deploy, cache purge, or regional ISP issue.
(b) Compare p95 to **CDN analytics** (CloudFront, Fastly, Akamai) for the same path/edge POP country.
(c) **Spot-check `status`:** high latency with mass **500/502** is origin errors, not “slow pages” — add `| where status < 500` if you want UX-only signal.
(d) Confirm **hit count** includes only customer traffic after bot filter replay.
(e) For one country, compare **median vs p95** gap; huge gap implies long-tail assets or regional loss—note for content teams.
(f) If RUM exists, join on `uri_path` hour bucket and confirm server p95 tracks LCP degradation directionally.

Step 4 — Operationalize
**Dashboard:** Row 1 — bar **p95 by country**; Row 2 — **table** drilldown; Row 3 — **map**; Row 4 — **scatter** hits vs p95 (see `visualization`).
**Alerting:** Route **checkout** and **cart** URI regex matches to **Commerce on-call** (PagerDuty service `retail-digital`); informational-only to **Slack #web-perf** for content paths.
**Runbook:** (1) Confirm incident not CDN misconfig. (2) Check last code deploy from `index=changes` or CI feed. (3) Roll back feature flag or purge edge cache. (4) Open ticket with **asset optimisation** if static weight spiked. (5) Post-mortem: add unit test on `response_time` parsing if root cause was **log format** drift.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Zero results always** — wrong `index`/`sourcetype`, or all p95 under threshold; run without `where` clause to see distribution.
• **Explosive p95 after TA upgrade** — Apache switched to `%D` microseconds without `props` normalisation; fix EVAL in `props.conf`.
• **Country = unknown** — GeoIP DB stale or private IPs; refresh **MaxMind** mmdb and verify `clientip` is public.
• **Alert spam on `/static/`** — exclude static asset paths via macro `retail_html_paths_only`.
• **Lag false negatives** — forwarder buffering; inspect `splunkd.log` on UF for ` TcpOutputProc` blockage.
• **Contradictory RUM vs access** — CDN served cache while origin slow; corroborate with cache **HIT/MISS** fields if logged.
grandmaExplanation: We spot which storefront pages crawl in which parts of the world. That helps the team fix sluggish screens where real shoppers browse, instead of guessing where online sales feel sluggish.
